Guideline: Polite register. Uses polite particles and formal constructions.
Thai is largely gender-neutral in professional contexts.
N/A for Thai script. English terms and acronyms in Latin characters.
Standard double quotes for feature names.
Thai uses spaces between clauses. Question marks for questions. Colons for labels.
Thai compound words without spaces between related terms. Transliterated English words common for technical terms.
Extensive Thai transliteration for technical terms (widget, check-in, feedback, chat, entity, field). Some kept in English: Analytics Hub, Data Foundation, PII, API, CSV, SFTP.
Thai verb forms for buttons. Instructions use polite request forms.
Enable: open-feature X pattern. Confirmations: are-you-sure pattern. Success: ...successfully pattern. Empty states: not-yet pattern.

Check-in=check-in (Thai); Review=assessment (Thai); Goal=target (Thai); Worker=employee (Thai); Manager=supervisor (Thai); Compensation=reward (Thai); Behavior=behavior (Thai); Career Development=career advancement (Thai); Settings=settings (Thai); Library=library (Thai transliteration)
